# DryUI — Agent Install Instructions

Zero-dependency Svelte 5 component library. Use the CLI to detect the project
and execute a tailored install plan.

## Step 1 — Run the install planner

```
npx -y @dryui/cli install --json
```

The command prints a single JSON object to stdout and exits. If the exit code
is non-zero, an error message is printed to stderr.

The JSON has two top-level fields:

- `detection` — project info (framework, packageManager, files, dependencies,
  theme state)
- `steps` — ordered array of actions to execute

Each step has:

- `kind`: `"install-package"` | `"edit-file"` | `"create-file"` | `"note"` | `"blocked"`
- `status`: `"pending"` | `"done"` | `"info"` | `"blocked"`
- `title` and `description`
- `command` (for install-package steps) — the shell command to run
- `path` and `snippet` (for edit-file / create-file steps)

Execute each `"pending"` step in order:

- `install-package` → Run the shell command in the `command` field from the
  project root directory.

- `create-file` → Create the file at `path` with the `snippet` as its full
  content. Create any intermediate directories if they don't exist.

- `edit-file` → Modify the existing file at `path` according to the
  `description`. The `snippet` shows the content to add or the target state.
  Read the `description` carefully — it specifies WHERE and HOW to apply the
  snippet (e.g., "prepend to the top" or "add to the existing <script> block").
  Do NOT blindly append or create duplicate elements.

- `note` / `info` → Read the `description` for guidance. No file changes needed.

- `blocked` → The step cannot proceed. Read the `description` to understand why
  and resolve the blocker before continuing.

If all steps have `status: "done"`, the project is already set up.

## Step 2 — Verify the setup

After executing all steps, confirm the setup succeeded:

```
npx -y @dryui/cli detect --json
```

The output should show `"status": "ready"`. If it shows `"partial"`, review
the `theme` and `dependencies` fields to identify what is still missing and
re-run Step 1.

## Step 3 — Set up the MCP server (recommended)

The DryUI MCP server provides tools for component lookup, composition guidance,
code review, theme diagnosis, and workspace health checks.

### Claude Code or generic MCP client (`.mcp.json` in project root)

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "dryui": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@dryui/mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Cursor (`.cursor/mcp.json`)

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "dryui": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@dryui/mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

### VS Code Copilot (`settings.json`)

```json
{
  "mcp": {
    "servers": {
      "dryui": {
        "command": "npx",
        "args": ["-y", "@dryui/mcp"]
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Codex (`.codex/config.toml`)

```toml
[mcp_servers.dryui]
command = "npx"
args = ["-y", "@dryui/mcp"]
```

If your editor is not listed, use `npx -y @dryui/mcp` with stdio transport.

## Step 4 — Use components

```svelte
<script>
  import { Button } from '@dryui/ui';
</script>

<Button variant="solid">Click me</Button>
```

Before building any UI, look up the component API first:

```
npx -y @dryui/cli info Button
npx -y @dryui/cli compose "login form"
```

## CLI tools (when MCP is not available)

If MCP is not available in your environment, use the CLI directly:

```
npx -y @dryui/cli info <component>      # Full API reference for a component
npx -y @dryui/cli list                   # All available components
npx -y @dryui/cli compose "query"        # Composition guidance for a UI task
npx -y @dryui/cli add --project <name>   # Project-aware component adoption plan
npx -y @dryui/cli review <file.svelte>   # Validate a Svelte file for spec compliance
npx -y @dryui/cli doctor                 # Workspace health check
```

Always look up a component with `info` before using it for the first time.
Compound components (Card, Field, Accordion, etc.) require `.Root` syntax —
e.g. `<Card.Root>` not `<Card>`.

## Full API reference

See https://dryui.dev/llms.txt for the complete component catalog, props,
theming tokens, and usage examples.
